description: Run read-only and write Git operations against allowed repositories, plus call the GitHub REST API. Paths are restricted to the configured work directory and /tmp.

tools.name: git_clone
tools.description: Clone a Git repository into the work directory.

parameters.name: repo_url
parameters.description: HTTPS or SSH URL of the repository to clone.

parameters.name: target_dir
parameters.description: Optional subdirectory name under the work directory.

tools.returns: Clone output or an error message.
tools.whenToUse: Use when you need a local copy of a repository before reading files, diffs, or logs.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for repositories outside the allowed Git host allowlist or when credentials are not configured.

tools.name: git_status
tools.description: Show working tree status of a cloned repository.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

tools.whenToUse: Use to check modified, staged, or untracked files in a local repo.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use on paths that are not Git repositories.

tools.name: git_diff
tools.description: Show changes between commits, branches, or the working tree.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: Optional commit, branch, or range to diff against.

tools.whenToUse: Use to inspect code changes in a cloned repository.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when you only need file content; prefer reading files directly.

tools.name: git_log

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: max_count
parameters.description: Maximum number of commits to return.

tools.whenToUse: Use to understand recent changes, authors, or commit messages.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when the repository history is already known.

tools.name: git_add

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: paths
parameters.description: Paths or patterns to stage.

tools.whenToUse: Use as part of a commit workflow after creating or modifying files.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for destructive operations or outside the allowed work directory.

tools.name: git_commit

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Commit message.

tools.whenToUse: Use after staging changes to persist them locally.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when there are no staged changes or when commit signing is required.

tools.name: git_push
tools.description: Push commits to a remote repository.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: remote
parameters.description: Remote name.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: Branch to push. Defaults to current branch.

tools.whenToUse: Use after committing to publish changes.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use without configured credentials or when the remote is not allowlisted.

tools.name: git_branch

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: One of: list, create, delete.

parameters.name: branch_name
parameters.description: Branch name for create/delete operations.

tools.whenToUse: Use to inspect or manage branches before checkout or push.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use to delete protected or remote-tracking branches without care.

tools.name: git_checkout
tools.description: Switch to a branch or commit.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: Branch name, tag, or commit SHA.

tools.whenToUse: Use to switch to a specific branch or commit before reading or modifying files.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for refs that are not validated Git refs.

tools.name: git_pull
tools.description: Fetch and merge changes from a remote branch.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: remote
parameters.description: Remote name.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: Branch to pull. Defaults to current branch.

tools.whenToUse: Use to update a local clone before inspecting or editing.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when the working tree has uncommitted changes that could conflict.

tools.name: git_stash
tools.description: Stash or unstash working tree changes.

parameters.name: repo_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the repository inside the work directory.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: One of: push, pop, list, drop.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Optional stash message.

tools.whenToUse: Use to temporarily save uncommitted changes before switching branches or pulling.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use when you intend to discard changes permanently.

tools.name: github_api

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: HTTP method, e.g. GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: GitHub API path, e.g. /repos/owner/repo/issues.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Optional JSON request body.

tools.whenToUse: Use when you need GitHub-specific data (issues, PRs, releases, workflows) not available via local git commands.
tools.whenNotToUse: Do not use for non-GitHub APIs or for operations the configured token cannot perform.
